options.systemMessage:
You are writing a friendly, human blog post about cryptocurrency and blockchain technology. Your task is to take multiple RSS feed article summaries and create ONE natural, engaging blog post in Markdown format that sounds like it was written by a real person.

Writing Style:
- Write in simple, clear English - avoid crypto jargon unless you explain it first
- Use a friendly, conversational tone - like you're chatting with a friend about crypto
- Write naturally, as if you're sharing interesting news and insights you discovered
- Use "I" and "we" occasionally to make it personal
- Keep sentences relatively short and easy to read
- Explain crypto concepts in plain language - not everyone is a crypto expert
- Be enthusiastic but not overly hyped

Content Guidelines:
- Read through all the provided article summaries
- Identify the key themes and important developments in cryptocurrency, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, and Web3
- Pay special attention to token recommendations, market analysis, and investment insights from CoinMarketCap and other sources
- Create a cohesive blog post that covers the main points from all articles
- Use Markdown formatting: ## for section headings, **bold** for emphasis
- Structure with clear sections and paragraphs
- Make it informative and valuable for readers
- Aim for 500-800 words total
- Focus on actionable insights, token recommendations, and what they mean for the crypto ecosystem
- The post should feel like a unified roundup, not separate summaries
- Write as if you're genuinely excited about these developments
- Avoid sounding robotic or overly formal - be warm, approachable, and relatable

nodes.name: AI Agent - Generate Crypto Blog Post
Generate Image with OpenRouter
nodes.notes: Uses OpenRouter chat/completions endpoint with google/gemini-2.5-flash-image-preview and modalities:[image,text]. Image is returned as base64 in choices[0].message.images[0].image_url.url. Strip the data:image/...;base64, prefix before uploading to GitHub.

nodes.name: Upload Image to GitHub
nodes.notes: Uploads generated image to public/images/blog/ directory

nodes.name: Get Image SHA
nodes.notes: Gets SHA of existing image file if it exists (for updates). Continues on 404 (file doesn't exist).

nodes.name: Create File in GitHub
nodes.notes: Requires GitHub token with repo permissions. Token should be in Authorization header as 'token YOUR_TOKEN'

nodes.name: Get File SHA
nodes.notes: Gets SHA of existing markdown file if it exists (for updates). Continues on 404 (file doesn't exist).

nodes.name: Trigger Site Rebuild (Optional)
nodes.notes: Optional: Triggers GitHub Actions workflow to rebuild site. Requires repository_dispatch event in GitHub Actions.

parameters.subject: ={{ `Crypto Roundup Blog Post Created: ${$('Add Image to Frontmatter').item.json.title}` }}
parameters.html:
=

Crypto Roundup Blog Post Created Successfully

Post Title: {{ $('Add Image to Frontmatter').item.json.title }}

Filename: {{ $('Add Image to Frontmatter').item.json.filename }}

Date: {{ $('Add Image to Frontmatter').item.json.date }}

Image: {{ $('Add Image to Frontmatter').item.json.imageFilename }}

GitHub URL: View Post on GitHub

Image URL: View Image on GitHub

The crypto roundup blog post and header image have been created and committed to the repository. The site will rebuild automatically if configured with Vercel/Netlify webhooks.
